section_title: INTRODUCTION TO NASDAQ FUTURES AND WEEKLY CHART SETUP
content:
This session reviews the NASDAQ futures contract for September 2023's delivery. We begin by examining
the weekly chart, noting the previous week's low and high points.

- WEEKLY RANGE RETRACEMENT
A proprietary concept called TGIF is introduced. This setup is primarily based on a retracement to 20 to 30
percent of the weekly range. This concept has been utilized since 1998 and predates its public release on
YouTube.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING TGIF AND POWER OF 3 FOR WEEKLY HIGHS
content:
The TGIF setup, while not found in conventional trading literature, aligns with the Power of 3 concept. For a
bullish week, once a tradable high is formed or anticipated to be the potential high of the week, one can
expect a retracement back down into 20 to 30 percent of that weekly range.

section_title: CHALLENGING MARKET RANDOMNESS AND SUPPLY/DEMAND DYNAMICS
content:
TGIF stands for 'Thank God it's Friday', signifying a market that moves in one direction all week, then
retraces. This concept serves to challenge the arguments that markets are random or solely driven by
supply and demand. The precision taught demonstrates elements beyond simple statistical probability,
refuting the idea that markets are random when 90 percent of traders lose money.

- SELL-SIDE IMBALANCE AND CONSEQUENT
ENCROACHMENT
Transitioning to the daily chart, a shaded area is identified as a 'sell-side imbalance buy-side
inefficiency' (SBI/BISI). This is measured from a specific candle's high to another candle's low midpoint,
defining the 'consequent encroachment' (CE). The market was expected to trade up to this CE level on
Friday.

section_title: PRE-MARKET OPEN AND INITIAL PRICE ACTION ON ONE-MINUTE CHART
content:
The daily CE level correlates with a specific level on the one-minute chart. Prior to the stock market's
opening bell, all the highs were run. Following the opening, the market dropped, creating a fair value gap.
Price then traded up once more before dropping lower and taking out a specific low.

- BUY-SIDE AND SELL-SIDE ENGINEERING
The objective was to see a specific low taken out. This area held 'buy-side' liquidity, which are buy stops
typically targeted by breakout traders using trend lines. The strategy involved taking the buy-side first, then
running the 'sell-side relative equal lows'. This precise sequence was observed in price action.

section_title: TRADE ENTRY AND TRANSITION TO LIVE EXECUTION
content:
In a previous interactive study, viewers were prompted to identify the trade's underlying methodology. The
market traded up into a fair value gap, marking the entry point. Price then broke down into a buy-side
imbalance sell-side inefficiency (BISI). The discussion transitions from a hindsight review to a live trade
execution, utilizing a real money account with a CFTC-regulated broker in the U.S.

section_title: ANTICIPATED PRICE DELIVERY AND LOWER TARGETS
content:
The expectation for the BISI was that it would be entirely disrespected, meaning price would trade
completely through it, overlapping the entire range. The goal was for price to run directly down into the sell
- side liquidity, reaching the low end of the daily shaded area identified earlier on the one-minute chart.

section_title: PRICE DELIVERY, TGIF OBJECTIVE MET, AND FINAL TARGET CONFIRMATION
content:
Speed in price delivery was anticipated and observed. The ultimate low objective for the weekly range
retracement, based on the TGIF setup, was 15455. The market's low reached 15454, one handle below the
target, trading inside a fair value gap in the form of a buy-side imbalance sell-side inefficiency, just below
Thursday's low. This specific price level (15460.25) was anticipated and tweeted about before Friday.

section_title: LIVE ACCOUNT VERIFICATION AND SHORT ENTRY
content:
Addressing requests for live trading demonstrations, this segment confirms a live account with AMP
Futures. The short entry was executed as price traded up into the fair value gap, shaded in pink on the
right-hand side chart.

section_title: STOP LOSS PLACEMENT AND SMT DIVERGENCE CONFIRMATION
content:
The stop loss was placed just above the longest wick, specifically above the midpoint of that wick, which is
the consequent encroachment (CE). This placement implies confidence that price will not revisit that level.
Further confirmation for the short bias came from SMT (Smart Money Technique) Divergence, where
NASDAQ failed to make a higher high, contrasting with a higher high formed on the S&P.

section_title: REAL-TIME EXECUTION CONTEXT AND PRIOR CHALLENGE
content:
The video is presented in real-time, unsped, replicating the actual trading experience. This trade follows a
Twitter Space discussion from a day or two prior to Friday, where a challenge was made regarding the
model's ability to deliver a $15,000 trade on Non-Farm Payrolls Friday, with doubts about its occurrence
outside of paper trading.

section_title: ANTICIPATING BISI DISRESPECT AND VERACITY OF LIVE TRADE
content:
The trade continues with the anticipation that the buy-side imbalance sell-side inefficiency (BISI) will be
completely disrespected and price will run through it. It is emphasized that stating this is live fund trading if
it were not would be illegal, underscoring the authenticity and integrity of the live trade demonstration,
which students have witnessed many times before.

- STOP TO BREAK-EVEN AND FULL PULL STRATEGY
As price accelerates through the low end of the shaded area, the stop loss is moved to better than break
- even. The strategy employed is a 'full pull', meaning no partial profit taking; the trade is held from full entry
to full target. The objective is for price to trade down into the sell-side liquidity, fulfilling the promise of a
$15,000 profit in a real account.

section_title: REVIEW OF TAUGHT CONCEPTS AND SUCCESSFUL TRADE EXECUTION
content:
The methodology used for this successful trade is reiterated as previously taught concepts, available for
free in the 'Model 2022' playlist combined with the TGIF video on the YouTube channel. As price trades
below the target, the trade is closed, demonstrating a $15,400 profit on a live account, achieved by
applying the free educational content provided.
